title: The Review of Religions, Volume VIII, No. 12
publisher: The Manager, Review of Religions, Qadian
notes: Ahmadiyya Review of Religions, Dec 1909 (closes Vol VIII). Periodical.

title: The Review of Religions, Volume IX, No. 1
publisher: The Manager, Review of Religions, Qadian
notes: Ahmadiyya Review of Religions, Jan 1910 (Vol IX opens) — current-quarter issue. Periodical.

title: Punjabi Bhain
gloss: The Punjabi Sister, Volume 3, Nos. 7 and 8, for November and December, 1909. A monthly magazine devoted to the interests of female education.
notes: 'Punjabi Bhain' (The Punjabi Sister) — the pioneering GURMUKHI women's magazine of Bhai Takht Singh, the Sikh women's-education crusader of Ferozepore (founder of the Sikh Kanya Mahavidyala); a landmark of Sikh female-education reform and of Punjabi (as opposed to Urdu) periodical culture. pencil 'Specimen' in left margin. Directly on-theme: vernacular-language women's-reform press, and the Punjabi-language self-assertion. Periodical.

title: Punjabi Bhain, Nos. 4 and 5
gloss: Nos. 4 and 5, for September and October, 1909. A monthly magazine devoted to the interests of female education.
notes: Earlier double-number of Punjabi Bhain (Sept-Oct 1909), registered here — the back-numbers catching up in the register. Periodical.

printer_verbatim: The 'Civil and Military Gazette' Press, Lahore.
title: The Urdu Punjab Record, Part II, Volume XLIV, No. 1
gloss: Executive, for the quarter ending March, 1909. Edited by Mr Ganpat Rai, Barrister-at-Law.
copyright: The Proprietors, 'Civil and Military Gazette' Press, Lahore; No. 8, dated the 28th January, 1910.
notes: The URDU edition of the Punjab Record law-reports — the colonial legal-reporting apparatus issuing a vernacular (Urdu) parallel, edited by an Indian barrister (Ganpat Rai); the bilingual doubling of the official law-report is a precise artefact of legal knowledge being vernacularized for native practitioners (cf. the Urdu statute-translations at p30). Periodical. Composite pagination.

title: The Urdu Punjab Record, Part I, Volume XLIV, No. 8
